entries.definition:
j–th–w/y rock-pile, mound, a small hill; to kneel; body; a grave mound. Of this root, two words occur three times in the Qurʾan: جَاثِيَة jāthiyatun once and جِثِيّ jithiyy twice.
جَاثِيَة jāthiyatun [fem. act. part.] kneeling alert and ready to be called; kneeling down in humility (45:28) وَتَرَىٰ كُلَّ أُمَّةٍ جَاثِيَةً كُلُّ أُمَّةٍ تُدْعَىٰ إِلَىٰ كِتَابِهَا you will see every community kneeling: every community will be summoned to its record; * الجاثية name of Sura

156

j–th–w/y rock-pile, mound, a small hill; to kneel; body; a grave mound. Of this root, two words occur three times in the Qurʾan: جَاثِيَة jāthiyatun once and جِثِيّ jithiyy twice.
جَاثِيَة jāthiyatun [fem. act. part.] kneeling alert and ready to be called; kneeling down in humility (45:28) وَتَرَىٰ كُلَّ أُمَّةٍ جَاثِيَةً كُلُّ أُمَّةٍ تُدْعَىٰ إِلَىٰ كِتَابِهَا you will see every community kneeling: every community will be summoned to its record; * الجاثية name of Sura

156
